title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Supporting sources are placeholders (e.g., source_001) rather than primary authorities; claims lack citations to specific state statutes, state supreme/court of appeals decisions, or identified federal rules or opinions.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Time‑sensitivity is noted but not operationalized: no dates for last authoritative change, no citation to pending federal litigation or enacted/struck federal rules (e.g., FTC rule developments), leaving practical reliability unquantified.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Claim c001: Good framing but lacks citation to controlling primary authorities—supporting_sources are placeholders (source_001, etc.) rather than specific state statutes, state supreme/court of appeals decisions, or identified federal statutes/agency rules; time-sensitivity noted but not dated.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Claim c002: Identifies relevant follow‑up questions but fails to specify how to determine which jurisdiction 'controls' (choice‑of‑law rules) or whether federal preemption applies; contradictions should be scoped to a particular jurisdiction before left 'unresolved.'

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Claims rely on high-level descriptions without citing controlling primary authorities (specific state statutes and leading state high-court or court-of-appeals decisions); add jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction citations.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Reference to “recent federal action (statute or agency rule)” is too vague—identify the specific federal statute or agency rule and its current legal status (e.g., challenged, enjoined, vacated) and cite primary federal decisions or dockets.
